result.meeting_context: Council Policy and Procedures Committee met on December 15 via video under the state of emergency. The agenda focused on amendments to Council Policy K2/Code of Conduct (Form 700 compliance and application deadlines), a 2023 work plan update, and a request to clarify processes for private developers using public land.

points.speaker: Lisa Matichak
points.text: Committee approved the September 28, 2022 meeting minutes with a unanimous vote following a motion by Lucas Ramirez and second by Alison Hicks. No committee member or public raised substantive questions regarding the minutes before the vote.
points.source: Lisa Matichak : Yes, that passes unanimously.

topics.topic: Council Policy K2 and Code Amendments

points.speaker: Heather Glaser
points.text: Staff proposed amending the Code of Conduct and Council Policy K2 to suspend advisory body members who fail to file Form 700 until a proper filing is submitted, with any missed meetings during suspension counted as unexcused. Additional minor cleanup edits were included.
points.source: Heather Glaser : ...would suspend a member's participation on the body to which they're appointed until a proper filing is made. And we're recommending that those missed meetings during that... are qualified as unexcused.

points.speaker: Heather Glaser
points.text: To address varying term expirations and practical constraints, staff recommended removing prescriptive month-based timelines (e.g., five, four, two-to-three months) from Policy K2 procedures and adding language setting application deadlines as near as practicable to the interview date to ensure packets can be compiled reliably.
points.source: Heather Glaser : ...we could add a little language there and just ... that the deadline for applications should be set as near as practicable to the date, interviews will be held by the recommending body.

points.speaker: Lucas Ramirez
points.text: The committee advanced staff’s recommendations, moving to approve the Form 700 suspension policy and the application deadline language changes. Alison Hicks expressed concern about potential DRC impacts and voiced mixed support; roll call responses were inconsistent, but discussion indicates majority support to forward changes to Council.
points.source: Lucas Ramirez : Well, I'm I'm happy to move the staff recommendation and include the revisions that were just read into the record.

points.speaker: Kimbra McCarthy
points.text: City Manager flagged a risk that the Development Review Committee, primarily composed of architects, may face difficulty recruiting members willing to file Form 700s. Staff is evaluating options, including shifting to consultant-like arrangements, though filers may still be required; this challenge could affect the DRC’s viability.
points.source: Kimbra McCarthy : ...it is absolutely going to be a challenge for that Commission... So we're rethinking how that is done.

points.speaker: Heather Glaser
points.text: Policy provides that advisory bodies determine whether absences are excused or unexcused by general consent or majority vote, with the chair proposing a status in consultation with the staff liaison for inclusion in minutes. For City Council, excused absences are determined by the mayor under the City Charter.
points.source: Heather Glaser : ...the policy provides that each advisory body would determine by general consent or a majority vote whether the status of an absence is excused or unexcused.

points.speaker: Kimberly Thomas
points.text: Staff reported multiple work plan items completed. For 2023, two items are slated: Council Policy A2 minor cleanup in coordination with Finance, and further modifications to Council Policy A15 (Shoreline ticket policy) to be developed by Community Services, anticipated for spring.
points.source: Kimberly Thomas : ...two items that would be beyond this update... Council policy A2... and you also have a council policy a 15, which is the Shoreline ticket policy... there are some additional modifications... sometime in the spring.

topics.topic: Use of Public Land Process Clarification

points.speaker: Kimbra McCarthy
points.text: In response to a request to clarify processes for private developers seeking to use public land, the City Manager agreed to prepare an off‑agenda memo for the Council outlining current decision pathways, with potential future discussion on whether to codify a policy and the appropriate forum.
points.source: Kimbra McCarthy : ...it's absolutely doable for us to prepare at least for now an off agenda memo for council just so that you all can understand right now just the context of of decision making.

points.speaker: Lisa Matichak
points.text: A public commenter highlighted that the Advisory Body Handbook (late 2021) includes PSAB but is inconsistent on whether non‑residents may serve on various committees. The chair advised submitting an email to the City Council so the City Clerk can provide a clarifying response.
points.source: Lisa Matichak : I do still think sending an e-mail Mr. England is the right way to go because then our City Clerk can answer your question regarding, I think, the the handbook.

points.speaker: Lisa Matichak
points.text: The chair adjourned the meeting at 4:17 PM and thanked participants. Staff and committee members exchanged holiday wishes at adjournment.
points.source: Lisa Matichak : Then we will adjourn the meeting at 4:17.

decisions.text: Revise Council Policy K2 to remove month-based timelines and add application deadlines set near interview dates, and forward amended policy (including Form 700 suspension provision) to the City Council.
decisions.source: Lucas Ramirez : Well, I'm I'm happy to move the staff recommendation and include the revisions that were just read into the record.

decisions.text: Prepare an off‑agenda memo to the City Council explaining current processes for private developers proposing to use public land in projects.
decisions.source: Kimbra McCarthy : ...it's absolutely doable for us to prepare at least for now an off agenda memo for council just so that you all can understand right now just the context of of decision making.
